page.content: In the analysis of site-specific class association rules, the table presents a comprehensive overview of various methods employed to derive insights from the data. The metrics of Support and Confidence are particularly critical in evaluating the strength of these association rules. Support indicates the proportion of instances in the dataset that meet the criteria of the rule, while Confidence reflects the reliability of the inference made by the rule based on the data available. For example, the Wi-Fi method demonstrates a Support metric of 92.6%, suggesting a strong correlation between the variables analyzed.

lines.text: In the analysis of site-specific class association rules, the table presents a comprehensive overview of various methods
employed to derive insights from the data. The metrics of Support and Confidence are particularly critical in evaluating the

page.content: This high Support value indicates that the rule applies to a significant portion of the dataset, enhancing its relevance for practical applications. Additionally, the Confidence level of 1.85% indicates the likelihood that the conclusion drawn from this rule will hold true when the rule is applied in similar contexts. Such metrics are essential for practitioners aiming to leverage these rules for decision-making processes, as they provide a quantitative basis for understanding the relationships within the data. Overall, the insights derived from these association rules can significantly contribute to optimizing site-specific strategies and improving operational efficiencies.
